description: Recreate Galileo Galilei's view of Jupiter from Padua, Italy (45.4064° N, 11.8768° E, 12m) on January 7 and January 13, 1610, at 21:00 UTC. Simulate a clear telescopic view by disabling the atmosphere and ground, and enabling planet labels. Save this configuration. Capture screenshots of Jupiter and its moons for both dates. Create an exhibit label at /home/ga/Desktop/galileo_exhibit.txt containing the location "Padua", the year "1610", and the names of the four moons: Io, Europa, Ganymede, and Callisto.
